reasoning:
The historical trend for Commercial Real Estate (CRE) loans at US Commercial Banks has been generally upward. Recent available data from Q1 2024 shows continued growth despite systemic market stress, as banks have often chosen to delay foreclosures and work with borrowers—thereby retaining or even capitalizing loan balances on their books. Given this sticky, positive trajectory, short-term weekly changes lean toward the positive side, though they are subject to routine noise. I set the initial 1-week forecast at 65%. 

Longer horizons for a random walk with a positive drift typically feature increasing likelihoods of exceeding the current value. However, the system instructions explicitly mandate that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates must converge closer to 0.5. To strictly comply with this structural requirement, I have regressed the probabilities toward 0.5 for the subsequent resolution dates (0.60 for one month, and 0.55 for three months out).
batch_queries: Will the amount of money representing all commercial real estate loans made by commercial banks in the US have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
